title: Between Airports and Hotels
content: Frequent travel created a strange sense of familiarity with places that were never truly home. Ethan spent countless hours moving between airports, hotels, taxis, and train stations. Over time, different cities began sharing common features: identical hallways, similar reception desks, and familiar routines repeated across continents. Yet within that repetition, small differences remained important. A conversation with a hotel employee, the view from a window, or the atmosphere of a neighborhood could transform an otherwise ordinary stay into something memorable. Ethan realized that travel was often defined less by famous attractions and more by the countless small experiences connecting one destination to the next.
